Title: Successful login with valid credentials
Preconditions:
- User account exists with username "testuser" and password "SecurePass123".
- System is online and accessible.
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the login page.
2. User enters "testuser" in the username field.
3. User enters "SecurePass123" in the password field.
4. User clicks the "Login" button.
Expected Result:
- System authenticates the user and returns "Authenticated".
- User is redirected to the profile page or dashboard.

Title: Failed login with incorrect password
Preconditions: - User account exists with username "testuser" and password "SecurePass123".
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the login page.
2. User enters "testuser" in the username field.
3. User enters "wrongpassword" in the password field.
4. User clicks the "Login" button.
Expected Result:
- System returns "Unauthorized".
- User remains on the login page with an error message (e.g., "Invalid credentials").

Title: Failed login with non-existent username
Preconditions: - No user account exists with username "nonexistentuser".
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the login page.
2. User enters "nonexistentuser" in the username field.
3. User enters "anything" in the password field.
4. User clicks the "Login" button.
Expected Result:
- System returns "Unauthorized".
- User remains on the login page with an error message (e.g., "Invalid credentials").

Title: Login with empty username field
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the login page.
2. User leaves the username field empty.
3. User enters "SecurePass123" in the password field.
4. User clicks the "Login" button.
Expected Result:
- System validates input and returns an error (e.g., "Username is required").
- User remains on the login page.

Title: Login with empty password field
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the login page.
2. User enters "testuser" in the username field.
3. User leaves the password field empty.
4. User clicks the "Login" button.
Expected Result:
- System validates input and returns an error (e.g., "Password is required").
- User remains on the login page.

Title: Successful user registration with valid email and password
Preconditions:
- System allows new registrations.
- Email "newuser@example.com" is not already registered.
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the signup page.
2. User enters "newuser@example.com" in the email field.
3. User enters "NewPass456" in the password field.
4. User clicks the "Register" button.
Expected Result:
- System registers the user and returns "User registered successfully".
- User is redirected to the login page or profile setup.

Title: Registration with already existing email
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the signup page.
2. User enters "existinguser@example.com" in the email field.
3. User enters "NewPass456" in the password field.
4. User clicks the "Register" button.
Expected Result:
- System returns an error (e.g., "Email already in use").
- User remains on the signup page.

Title: Registration with invalid email format
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the signup page.
2. User enters "invalid.email" in the email field.
3. User enters "NewPass456" in the password field.
4. User clicks the "Register" button.
Expected Result:
- System validates input and returns an error (e.g., "Please enter a valid email").
- User remains on the signup page.

Title: Registration with empty password field
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the signup page.
2. User enters "newuser@example.com" in the email field.
3. User leaves the password field empty.
4. User clicks the "Register" button.
Expected Result:
- System validates input and returns an error (e.g., "Password is required").
- User remains on the signup page.

Title: Successful profile update with valid data
Preconditions:
- User is logged in as "testuser".
- User’s current profile data is stored.
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the profile page.
2. User enters updated profile data (name, bio, location).
3. User clicks the "Update Profile" button.
Expected Result:
- System updates the profile and returns "Profile updated successfully".
- Updated data is reflected on the profile page.

Title: Profile update with empty profile data
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the profile page.
2. User leaves all profile fields empty.
3. User clicks the "Update Profile" button.
Expected Result:
- System validates input and returns an error (e.g., "Profile data cannot be empty").
- User remains on the profile page with existing data intact.

Title: Profile update with invalid username format
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the profile page.
2. User attempts to change username to "invalid user".
3. User enters other profile data.
4. User clicks the "Update Profile" button.
Expected Result:
- System validates input and returns an error (e.g., "Username must be alphanumeric").
- User remains on the profile page with existing data intact.

Title: Successful session exit with valid reason
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the exit page.
2. User selects "Completed tasks" as the exit reason.
3. User clicks the "Exit" button.
Expected Result:
- System logs the exit reason and terminates the session.
- User is redirected to the login page with a confirmation message (e.g., "You have exited successfully").

Title: Session exit with empty reason
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the exit page.
2. User leaves the exit reason field empty.
3. User clicks the "Exit" button.
Expected Result:
- System validates input and returns an error (e.g., "Please select an exit reason").
- User remains on the exit page.

Title: Session exit without logging out (edge case)
Preconditions:
- User is logged in as "testuser".
- System does not enforce immediate session termination (assumed from lack of UML constraints).
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the exit page.
2. User selects "Technical issue" as the exit reason.
3. User clicks the "Exit" button.
4. User refreshes the browser (simulating a forced disconnect).
Expected Result:
- System logs the exit reason but does not enforce session termination.
- User remains logged in (or is logged out gracefully; clarify with stakeholders if critical).
- No error occurs during refresh.
